denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 04/02/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100546411          Group #: GRP-1069
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-113474
Claim #: CLM-2026-191658
Date of Service (requested): 03/13/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Central States Medical Associates

Dear Plan Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 03/15/2026 for MRI shoulder without contrast related to Shoulder instability MRI (M25.311). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: MRI shoulder without contrast
Diagnosis (as submitted): Shoulder instability MRI (M25.311)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied:
Clinical criteria applied: InterQual criteria (2018) and/or older MCG modules (superseded guideline reference). CPB 0129 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CPB 0129 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. A lower level of care is appropriate for Shoulder instability MRI (M25.311); MRI shoulder without contrast exceeds what is required. Outpatient management has not been exhausted per clinical guidelines.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the MCG Care Guidelines (edition not specified) and utilization-management requirements associated with level of care too high. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with an Aetna medical director to review clinical information before you file a written appeal.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100546411) and reference UM-2026-113474. 

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department
clinical_context: The 49-year-old member has Shoulder instability MRI (M25.311). He has been treated under commercial coverage with MRI shoulder without contrast recommended as the next appropriate step. Recurrent subluxation; exam apprehension positive. Submitted documentation supports medical necessity for MRI shoulder without contrast in the setting of Shoulder instability MRI (M25.311); the denial letter does not discuss those records or cite the objective findings in the file.
